examples: lodge a bullet in the table
stick your thumb in the crack
We are staying in Detroit; we are not moving to Cincinnati
Stay put in the corner here!
Stick around and you will learn something!
Will this wallpaper adhere to the wall?
The door sticks--we will have to plane it
The label stuck to her for the rest of her life

meanings.definition: be a devoted follower or supporter
examples: The residents of this village adhered to Catholicism
She sticks to her principles
She stood by her husband in times of trouble
The friends stuck together through the war

meanings.definition: cover and decorate with objects that pierce the surface
examples: stick some feathers in the turkey before you serve it

meanings.definition: fasten with an adhesive material like glue
examples: stick the poster onto the wall

meanings.definition: fasten with or as with pins or nails
examples: stick the photo onto the corkboard

meanings.definition: fasten into place by fixing an end or point into something
examples: stick the corner of the sheet under the mattress

meanings.definition: pierce with a thrust using a pointed instrument
examples: he stuck the cloth with the needle

meanings.definition: pierce or penetrate or puncture with something pointed
examples: He stuck the needle into his finger

meanings.definition: come or be in close contact with; stick or hold together and resist separation
examples: The dress clings to her body
The label stuck to the box
The sushi rice grains cohere

meanings.definition: saddle with something disagreeable or disadvantageous
examples: They stuck me with the dinner bill
I was stung with a huge tax bill

meanings.definition: be a mystery or bewildering to
examples: This beats me!
Got me--I don't know the answer!
a vexing problem
This question really stuck me
stuck in the mud
this problem has me completely stuck
